summary: I was born in Somalia and it is my homeland. I left Somalia in 2005 and have not been back for ten years. I miss my country deeply and hope to visit it someday when I become a citizen. Somalia is where my family, including my sisters and brothers, still live, and I always remember it fondly.

After leaving Somalia in 2005, I came to Kenya as a refugee and stayed in a refugee camp for ten years. It was in Kenya that I married my husband. Although I could not work while in Africa, Kenya was an important place for me during that time before I moved to America.

I arrived in the United States on July 23, 2013, after spending ten years in Kenya. When I first came, I thought I wouldn't find Somali food here, but I was pleasantly surprised. America has given me a better life; I am working and going to school, and my husband is doing the same. I brought a Muslim prayer rug from Kenya because I pray five times a day and thought I couldn't find one here.

When I first came to the United States, I stayed in Boston for two months before moving to Minnesota. Boston was my initial home in America, but I only stayed there briefly as I settled more permanently elsewhere.

After leaving Boston, I moved to Minnesota where I currently live. Minnesota has become my new home in America, where I work and go to school alongside my husband. Life here is good, and I have been able to build a stable life for my family.
